symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe perianal pain immediately after straining

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperacute severe pain from an external clot with straining as the precipitant is characteristic.
Fissures also cause severe perianal pain, but classically tearing pain on defecation rather than a sudden lump.

symptoms.symptom_name: New hard, tense, tender external perianal lump

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A discrete hard external lump appearing acutely is the hallmark of external haemorrhoidal thrombosis.
A tender perianal lump is shared with an abscess; the two cannot be separated on history alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Background intermittent painless bright red rectal bleeding on toilet paper

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Establishes pre-existing haemorrhoidal disease as the substrate for acute thrombosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic pruritus ani and sensation of incomplete emptying

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific haemorrhoidal symptoms supporting chronic haemorrhoidal disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reducible prolapsing anal tissue on defecation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolapsing internal haemorrhoidal tissue indicates established haemorrhoidal disease.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: prolapsed/incarcerated internal haemorrhoid or rectal prolapse
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raises rectal prolapse as an alternative, distinguished on examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Straining and heavy lifting during chronic constipation as precipitant

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised intra-abdominal pressure precipitates external haemorrhoidal thrombosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, chills and purulent discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lowers the probability of an abscess but does not exclude an early/small one — palpation for fluctuance is still needed.
A systemically well, afebrile patient fits a bland venous thrombosis rather than perianal sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of urinary retention (passing urine normally)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring against extensive perianal sepsis; retention would mandate emergency assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tense 1.5 cm bluish-purple subcutaneous nodule at the anal verge with no surrounding erythema, fluctuance or induration

investigation_details.expected_result: Discrete tense bluish-purple external nodule without abscess features
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot distinguish thrombosed external haemorrhoid from perianal abscess; risk of missed abscess.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tense bluish-purple skin-covered nodule at the anal verge without erythema/fluctuance is characteristic — but is a clinician inspection finding, sealed and unavailable via telehealth.
Absence of fluctuance, induration and spreading erythema on inspection argues against an abscess.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile clinician-measured vital signs (Temp 36.7, HR 90, BP 135/85, RR 16, SpO2 99%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever or tachycardia on formal measurement argues against systemic perianal sepsis — a clinician-measured value, sealed; the AI infers from patient-reported absence of fever, not a measured temperature.

symptoms.symptom_name: Digital rectal examination (deferred due to pain)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: DRE was deferred in person because external pathology was clear and the area too tender; noted as a limit, never performable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute external haemorrhoidal thrombosis on a background of haemorrhoidal disease
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Hyperacute painful external lump after straining, with a chronic haemorrhoidal substrate and a characteristic inspection finding, defines the diagnosis.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: perianal abscess (excluded on screen, pending exam)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A negative sepsis screen lowers abscess probability but does not replace the in-person inspection/palpation needed to exclude it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent same-day in-person perianal examination (inspection +/- gentle palpation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single essential step — distinguishes thrombosed external haemorrhoid from perianal abscess and guides whether clot evacuation is appropriate. Cannot be done via telehealth.
Not required for an afebrile, systemically well patient with a classic thrombosed external haemorrhoid; only if perianal sepsis is suspected on examination.
Not first-line; reserved for suspected complex/recurrent abscess or fistula after in-person assessment.
